title: Resident Technical Officer – M&E
description:
Company Bio


Morimatsu Pharmadule Singapore is a newly build company in Singapore at beginning of year 2023. Main business focus on modular facility delivery to pharmaceutical industry. The Morimatsu Group as mother company of Morimatsu Pharmadule Singapore has over 40 years history and $2 Billion USD Annual Turnover until now. Over 30 Years Experience in Modular design and Construction. As a leader in modular facility delivery, more than 80 modular plants have been provided, of which more than 70 are overseas projects. Morimatsu can provide turnkey solution to client starting from Engineering, Design, Purchasing, Construction to GMP Compliance and Validation for pharmaceutical industry.


Job Purpose:


As a site-based M&E RTO, you are responsible for supervising and coordinating mechanical and electrical works in pharmaceutical facility construction. The role requires strong understanding of M&E design drawings, regulatory compliance, and coordination with design teams to ensure installation meets specification, code, and GMP expectations.


Key Responsibilities


1. Drawing Interpretation & Design Coordination

  • Deep understanding of construction drawings, schematics, SLDs, and layout plans.
  • Proactively identify design conflicts or issues on-site and coordinate with designers for resolution.
  • Support RFIs and constructability reviews with the design team.

2. Regulatory Compliance & Submission Support


Familiar with and apply key local codes such as:

  • SCDF (Fire Protection/FSSD submissions)
  • PUB (Plumbing, condensate, chilled water drainage)
  • NEA (Exhaust systems, chemical handling)
  • Assist in preparing compliance documentation and test plans for FSC and TOP submissions.

3. Site Supervision & Quality Control

  • Supervise key installations including HVAC, fire protection, process piping, electrical, and control systems.
  • Review material deliveries, installation quality, and documentation to ensure conformance.
  • Support and witness SAT, TAB, and functional performance tests.

4.Coordination & Documentation

  • Maintain daily site diary, photo records, NCR logs, and rectification tracking.
  • Attend coordination meetings with contractors, designers, and client to resolve technical issues.

Requirements

  • Diploma or above in engineering disciplines, preferably mechanical or electrical.
  • Registered M&E RTO with BCA Singapore is mandatory.
  • Minimum 10 years of site experience, preferably in pharmaceutical or GMP projects.
  • Capable of reading and interpreting M&E drawings; experience in technical clarifications (RFI) is a plus.
  • Knowledge of SCDF, PUB, NEA submission and approval processes is required.
  • Familiar with FSC testing, TOP submission flow, and common system testing requirements.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in process/gas piping, high-voltage electrical, or BMS is an advantage.
  • GMP validation support experience (e.g., DQ/IQ/OQ coordination) is preferred.
  • Ability to use Navisworks or BIM viewers to assist in clash checking is a plus.
  • Coordinates closely with design teams, QPs, subcontractors, client, and authorities

Benefit:

  • 14days Annual leave
  • AWS
  • Phone Allowance
  • Medical Insurance

postedCompany.description:
Morimatsu Group has delivered projects to more than 40 countries/regions in the world, established an excellent industrial reputation in the process.

Morimatsu is dedicated in providing core equipment, process systems, integrated engineering solutions, modular plants, and digital operations for life sciences, fast-moving consumer goods, and electronics industries.

Our highly skilled professional teams, which include project managers, design engineers, validation experts, and operational technicians, continuously bringing clients the most advanced industrial concepts as well as cutting-edge technologies.
postedCompany.name: MORIMATSU PHARMADULE (SINGAPORE) PTE. LTD.
